title: Install Replacement Controller PCA In The Operator Station Control Box
summary: Install a replacement controller PCA into the operator station control box by transferring the plastic pieces and grounding wire, mounting the PCA, reconnecting the ribbon cable, Molex connectors, and FFC, remounting the control box, reinstalling panels as needed, and then restarting the operator station using the separately documented startup procedure.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a replacement controller PCA is being installed in the operator station control box as part of restoring the operator station after controller PCA replacement.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete controller PCA replacement procedure when removal steps are also required.
Do not use this runbook to perform the operator station restart itself without the separate documented 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66.
Do not use this runbook when the grounding wire, plastic pieces, retainer, ribbon cable, Molex connectors, or FFC cannot be installed as described.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the internal hardware installation, cable reconnection, control box remounting, panel reinstallation, and handoff to the documented restart procedure.

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked support-safe in the candidate and involves internal control box work and reconnection of multiple hardware components.
Make sure excess cable is fed back into the stand and do not pinch any wires when hanging and securing the control box.
Stop and escalate if any listed component cannot be installed or routed as described.
access_or_tools_needed: Fastener for grounding wire and plastic pieces
Four M3 socket-head screws for PCA mounting
Two M3 socket-head screws for retainer mounting
Top and/or bottom side panels, if removed
Access to the operator station restart procedure on page 66

access_required: Access to the operator station control box
Access to the documented 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66
system_preconditions: A replacement controller PCA is available for installation.
The control box is available to receive the replacement PCA.
Any removed top and/or bottom side panels are available for reinstallation if necessary.

visual_references.description: Flat-flex cable figure relevant to FFC handling and related clamp hardware.
ClearLink controller figure identifying the controller box location and mounting context.
Cable installation diagram useful for cable routing and connector handling context.

steps.title: Transfer plastic pieces and grounding wire to the new PCA
steps.purpose: Prepare the replacement PCA with the transferred hardware and grounding connection before mounting it in the control box.
steps.instruction: Transfer the two plastic pieces to the new PCA with the grounding wire in one corner, and use a fastener through all the parts to hold the grounding wire in place.
steps.expected_result: The two plastic pieces and grounding wire are installed on the new PCA and retained by the fastener.
steps.healthy_condition: The grounding wire is positioned in the correct corner and the fastener passes through all required parts so the assembly is held in place.
steps.failure_condition: The grounding wire, plastic pieces, or fastener cannot be positioned or secured as described.

actions.label: Transfer plastic pieces and grounding wire
actions.exact_action: Transfer the two plastic pieces to the new PCA with the grounding wire in one corner, and use a fastener through all the parts to hold the grounding wire in place.

stop_or_escalate_if: The grounding wire cannot be installed in the required corner.
The plastic pieces cannot be transferred or aligned.
The fastener cannot pass through all parts to hold the grounding wire in place.

source_evidence.text: Transfer the two plastic pieces to the new PCA with the grounding wire in one corner, and use a fastener through all the parts to hold the grounding wire in place.

steps.title: Mount the new PCA in the control box
steps.purpose: Secure the replacement PCA in its installed position inside the control box.
steps.instruction: Mount the new PCA in the box using the four M3 socket-head screws.
steps.expected_result: The replacement PCA is mounted in the control box using all four M3 socket-head screws.
steps.healthy_condition: The PCA is seated in position and secured with the four specified screws.
steps.failure_condition: The PCA cannot be positioned correctly or the four M3 socket-head screws cannot secure it.

actions.label: Mount replacement PCA
actions.exact_action: Mount the new PCA in the box using the four M3 socket-head screws.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller box location and mounting context for the controller assembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: The PCA cannot be mounted in the box.
Any of the four M3 socket-head screws cannot be installed.

source_evidence.text: Mount the new PCA in the box using the four M3 socket-head screws.
Figure 7-13: CLINK Controller ... identifies the controller or controller box referenced in the removal steps.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-13 appears on page 157 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_13_1_removal.

steps.title: Connect the ribbon cable and install the retainer
steps.purpose: Reconnect the ribbon cable and secure it with the retainer.
steps.instruction: Connect the ribbon cable and mount the retainer using the two M3 socket-head screws.
steps.expected_result: The ribbon cable is connected and the retainer is mounted with the two M3 socket-head screws.
steps.healthy_condition: The ribbon cable is connected at its intended point and the retainer is secured in place.
steps.failure_condition: The ribbon cable cannot be connected or the retainer cannot be mounted with the specified screws.

actions.label: Connect ribbon cable
actions.exact_action: Connect the ribbon cable and mount the retainer using the two M3 socket-head screws.

stop_or_escalate_if: The ribbon cable cannot be connected as described.
The retainer cannot be mounted with the two M3 socket-head screws.

source_evidence.text: Connect the ribbon cable and mount the retainer using the two M3 socket-head screws.

steps.title: Plug in the three Molex connectors
steps.purpose: Restore the Molex electrical connections to the replacement PCA assembly.
steps.instruction: Plug in the three Molex connectors.
steps.expected_result: All three Molex connectors are plugged in.
steps.failure_condition: One or more Molex connectors cannot be plugged in.

actions.label: Connect Molex connectors
actions.exact_action: Plug in the three Molex connectors.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any of the three Molex connectors cannot be plugged in as described.

source_evidence.text: Plug in the three Molex connectors.

steps.title: Plug in the FFC
steps.purpose: Reconnect the flat-flex cable to restore the required controller connection.
steps.healthy_condition: The FFC is connected at its intended connection point.
steps.failure_condition: The FFC cannot be plugged in as described.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Flat-flex cable appearance and related clamp hardware context.

stop_or_escalate_if: The FFC cannot be plugged in as described.

source_evidence.text: Figure 7-14 "Flat-Flex Cable" appears on page 161 ... installs the new FFC, and reinstalls the clamps.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-14 appears on page 161 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_14_1_replacement.

steps.title: Hang the control box on the stand and route cable safely
steps.purpose: Return the control box to its mounted position while protecting cable routing.
steps.instruction: Hang the control box on the M8 button-head screws on the stand, making sure to feed excess cable back into the stand and not pinch any wires.
steps.expected_result: The control box is hung on the stand and excess cable is routed back into the stand without pinched wires.
steps.healthy_condition: The control box is supported on the stand and cable routing does not pinch or trap any wires.
steps.failure_condition: The control box cannot be hung correctly or cables cannot be routed back into the stand without pinching wires.

actions.label: Hang control box on stand
actions.exact_action: Hang the control box on the M8 button-head screws on the stand, making sure to feed excess cable back into the stand and not pinch any wires.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller box location and stand mounting context.
General cable routing example to support avoiding pinched or poorly routed wires.
Cable routing and connector handling points relevant to feeding excess cable and avoiding wire damage.

stop_or_escalate_if: Cables cannot be routed back into the stand without pinching wires.
The control box cannot be hung on the M8 button-head screws as described.

source_evidence.text: Hang the control box on the M8 button-head screws on the stand, making sure to feed excess cable back into the stand and not pinch any wires.
Figure 7-13 ... identifies the controller or controller box location.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-13 appears on page 157 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_13_1_removal.

steps.title: Tighten the four M8 button-head screws
steps.purpose: Secure the controller box to the stand after it has been rehung.
steps.instruction: Tighten the four M8 button-head screws securing the controller box to the stand.
steps.expected_result: The controller box is secured to the stand by the four M8 button-head screws.
steps.healthy_condition: All four M8 button-head screws are tightened and the controller box is secured to the stand.
steps.failure_condition: One or more of the four M8 button-head screws cannot be tightened or the controller box remains unsecured.

actions.label: Secure controller box
actions.exact_action: Tighten the four M8 button-head screws securing the controller box to the stand.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller box mounting location for the stand attachment points.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any of the four M8 button-head screws cannot be tightened.
The controller box is not secure after tightening.

source_evidence.text: Tighten the four M8 button-head screws securing the controller box to the stand.
Nearby text ... loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box.

source_ref.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-13 appears on page 157 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_13_1_removal.

steps.title: Reinstall side panels if necessary
steps.purpose: Restore any removed guarding or access panels after the controller box installation is complete.
steps.instruction: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels, if necessary.
steps.expected_result: Any required top and/or bottom side panels are reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: Removed panels are back in place where needed.
steps.failure_condition: Required side panels cannot be reinstalled.

actions.label: Reinstall side panels
actions.exact_action: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels, if necessary.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any required top and/or bottom side panel cannot be reinstalled.

source_evidence.text: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels, if necessary.

steps.title: Restart the operator station using the documented startup procedure
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service readiness using the separately documented startup instructions.
steps.instruction: Restart the operator station using the documented 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is ready to be restarted using the referenced startup procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: Installation is complete and the procedure transitions to the documented startup process.
steps.failure_condition: The restart cannot proceed because the separate startup procedure is unavailable or installation issues remain unresolved.

actions.label: Use documented startup procedure
actions.exact_action: Restart the operator station using the documented 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66.

stop_or_escalate_if: The separate 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure is not available.
Installation issues remain unresolved before restart.

source_evidence.text: Restart the operator station using the documented 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure on page 66.

success_criteria: The replacement controller PCA is installed in the control box.
The two plastic pieces and grounding wire are transferred and secured.
The ribbon cable, three Molex connectors, and FFC are reconnected.
The control box is secured to the stand.
Excess cable is fed back into the stand without pinched wires.
Top and/or bottom side panels are reinstalled as needed.
The operator station is ready to proceed with the documented restart procedure.
healthy_conditions: Grounding wire and plastic pieces are secured on the new PCA.
The PCA is mounted with four M3 socket-head screws.
The ribbon cable and retainer are installed.
All three Molex connectors are plugged in.
The control box is hung and secured to the stand.
No wires are pinched during cable routing.
failure_conditions: The grounding wire, plastic pieces, retainer, ribbon cable, Molex connectors, or FFC cannot be installed as described.
Cables cannot be routed back into the stand without pinching wires.
The controller box cannot be secured to the stand.
The separate restart procedure is required but not included in this section.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the grounding wire, plastic pieces, retainer, ribbon cable, Molex connectors, or FFC cannot be installed as described.
Escalate if cables cannot be routed back into the stand without pinching wires.
Escalate if the controller box cannot be mounted or secured with the specified hardware.
Escalate if restart cannot proceed because the separate 'Starting The Operator Station' procedure is needed and not available.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: FFC appearance and related clamp hardware context.
Controller box location and mounting context.
General cable routing reference relevant to avoiding pinched wires.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide OCR text for pages 179-181, so step wording is grounded primarily in the candidate and source references rather than direct section text quotes.
No explicit torque values are provided in the packet for the M3 or M8 fasteners used in this installation section.
No explicit PPE list is provided in the candidate for this installation section.
The restart procedure content is not included in this packet; only the reference to page 66 is available.
No directly matching artifact in the packet explicitly shows the plastic pieces, grounding wire corner location, PCA screw locations, or ribbon cable retainer placement for this exact section.
